title: Treasury Secretary: The US could sanction Chinese AI companies
excerpt: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the Trump administration is looking into whether Chinese open source AI models were built on stolen IP.
content_html:
Treasury Secretary says it's 'unacceptable' that Chinese open source AI models may have been built on 'IP theft'

By Brent D. Griffiths

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

The Trump administration is signaling that it may clamp down on Chinese open-source AI models that are the talk of the industry.

On Tuesday, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US government is looking into whether popular open-source AI models from Chinese-based companies stole the intellectual property of their US competitors.

"There's a very technical AI word for it called distillation, but you and I would call it theft," Bessent said during an interview on Fox Business.

Bessent said the Trump administration welcomes open-sourced AI models in general, but will not tolerate companies that build their offerings on "IP theft."

"This administration supports open source models, but what we do not support is IP theft," he said. "And if we see, especially that overseas models are stealing from our great companies, we have the ability to sanction them because of this theft."

Bessent's comments are likely to be well received by AI giants like Anthropic, which previously accused Chinese companies like Moonshot AI, the maker of Kimi K3, Minimax, and DeepSeek, of essentially ripping off its AI research through a process known as distillation.

At the time, Anthropic said the Chinese companies accessed "Claude at scale while evading detection" and then used that access to conduct improper training that violated the company's terms of service.

Unlike Anthropic, Bessent did not name specific companies.

"We are finding watermarks of our US large language models on many Chinese models, and that's unacceptable," he said.

Moonshot AI's release of Kimi K3 has upended the generative AI race. Closely watched AI benchmarks show that the Chinese model performs functions such as coding at or above the level of Anthropic's Fable 5 or OpenAI's GPT-5.6-Sol, the two companies' current frontier, proprietary models.

Unlike proprietary models, open weight models are made available for users to download and to tweak at their leisure. Moonshot has promised to release Kimi 3's full weights on July 27. In the meantime, users are accessing it through the firm's website.

There is an intense debate brewing in tech over what, if anything, the US government should do in response to Kimi K3.

Dean Ball, a former senior advisor on AI to President Donald Trump who is newly OpenAI's head of strategy, faced intense pushback when he suggested that the White House may breed FUD ( fear, uncertainty, and doubt) to get the private sector to shy away from deploying Chinese open source models. Legendary tech investor Bill Gurley wrote a Washington Post op-ed on Monday that portrayed open-source models as one of the few ways the US could avoid an AI duopoly in which Anthropic and OpenAI maintain a stranglehold.

Anthropic has also faced skepticism from some quarters when it has complainbed about distillation attacks, given that its own AI models were built on copyrighted materials that authors and media companies have said were used without permission.

Bessent said that there are US companies pursuing open-source models, naming Meta, Nvidia, and Reflection AI. But for now, the Chinese companies are widely viewed as dominating the space.

"These models have to be held to the same standard as US models are, but more importantly, are they using stolen materials?" he said.

Read next

Brent D. Griffiths

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Brent Griffiths is a senior reporter at Business Insider who covers AI and tech.Previously, he worked at the Washington Post as a researcher on Power Up and the Finance 202. He started his career at Politico where he worked on the web production team and covered breaking news. His passion for covering politics has only grown since he cut his teeth covering the presidential campaign as a student journalist. He's also contributed to the Almanac of American Politics.

content_html_zh:
财政部长表示，中国开源人工智能模型可能是建立在“知识产权盗窃”基础上的，这是“不可接受的”

布伦特·D. Griffiths 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

特朗普政府发出信号，可能会打压业内热议的中国开源人工智能模型。

周二，美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特表示，美国政府正在调查中国公司流行的开源人工智能模型是否窃取了美国竞争对手的知识产权。

贝森特在接受福克斯商业频道采访时表示：“有一个非常技术性的人工智能词叫做蒸馏，但你和我会称之为盗窃。”

贝森特表示，特朗普政府总体上欢迎开源人工智能模型，但不会容忍以“盗窃知识产权”为基础构建产品的公司。

“本届政府支持开源模式，但我们不支持知识产权盗窃，”他说。 “如果我们看到，特别是海外模特正在从我们伟大的公司窃取信息，我们就有能力因为这种盗窃行为而制裁他们。”

Bessent 的言论可能会受到 Anthropic 等人工智能巨头的好评，Anthropic 此前曾指责 Moonshot AI（Kimi K3、Minimax 和 DeepSeek 的制造商）等中国公司通过一种称为蒸馏的过程实质上抄袭了其人工智能研究。

Anthropic 当时表示，这些中国公司“在逃避检测的同时大规模访问了克劳德”，然后利用该访问权限进行了违反公司服务条款的不当培训。

与 Anthropic 不同，Bessent 没有透露具体公司的名称。

“我们在许多中国模型上发现了美国大语言模型的水印，这是不可接受的，”他说。

Moonshot AI 发布的 Kimi K3 颠覆了生成式人工智能竞赛。 备受关注的人工智能基准测试表明，中国模型的编码等功能达到或高于 Anthropic 的 Fable 5 或 OpenAI 的 GPT-5.6-Sol（两家公司当前的前沿专有模型）的水平。

与专有模型不同，开放权重模型可供用户下载并在闲暇时进行调整。 Moonshot 承诺将于 7 月 27 日发布 Kimi 3 的全部重量。 与此同时，用户可以通过该公司的网站访问它。

科技界正在激烈争论美国政府应该采取什么措施来应对《Kimi K3》。

唐纳德·特朗普总统的前人工智能高级顾问、新任 OpenAI 战略主管迪恩·鲍尔 (Dean Ball) 表示，白宫可能会滋生 FUD（恐惧、不确定性和怀疑），让私营部门回避部署中国开源模型，但他遭到了强烈反对。 传奇科技投资者比尔·格利 (Bill Gurley) 周一在《华盛顿邮报》上发表了一篇专栏文章，将开源模型描述为美国可以避免人工智能双头垄断（Anthropic 和 OpenAI 保持束缚）的少数方法之一。

Anthropic 在抱怨蒸馏攻击时也受到了一些方面的质疑，因为它自己的人工智能模型是建立在受版权保护的材料上的，作者和媒体公司称这些材料是在未经许可的情况下使用的。

Bessent表示，美国有一些公司追求开源模型，名为Meta、Nvidia和Reflection AI。 但目前，人们普遍认为中国公司在该领域占据主导地位。

“这些模型必须遵守与美国模型相同的标准，但更重要的是，它们是否使用了偷来的材料？” 他说。

布伦特·格里菲斯 (Brent Griffiths) 是《商业内幕》(Business Insider) 的高级记者，报道人工智能和科技。此前，他曾在《华盛顿邮报》担任 Power Up 和 Finance 202 的研究员。 他的职业生涯始于 Politico，在网络制作团队工作并报道突发新闻。 自从他作为一名学生记者开始报道总统竞选以来，他对报道政治的热情与日俱增。 他还为《美国政治年鉴》做出了贡献。
